ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: In addition to the above, the tracks serving platforms 9 & 10 have 3rd rail conductors of 750 volts DC. All works undertaken on the station must be undertaken in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified lines. Please also note that portable metal steps and ladders <electrical conductors> are specifically banned from the station in order to avoid the danger of contact with traction supply equipment.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: There are numerous power cable runs of both high and low voltages above and below ground level throughout the station. Should there be any possibility whatsoever of cables being disturbed by work activities at a site, it will be necessary for contractors to establish the exact location and nature of cable runs prior to work commencing. This is in order that appropriate measures may be implemented to protect the safety of personnel, and the integrity of the cables and other equipment. Requirement to disturb the surface within the station's controlled area, the Buried Services Procedure shall be followed at all times. Work will not be allowed to commence until such time that a Permit to Dig/Penetrate form has been duly signed by the Contract Administrator.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: In addition to the above cabling, Euston station contains a large quantity of operational equipment including Signalling and Telecommunications <S&T> cables and train dispatch equipment. Any contractor proposing to undertake any demolition, excavation or works that could disturb any cables should refer to the National Hazard Directory. Additional information on S&T equipment can be obtained from the Route Signalling Engineer.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: A Lightning Protection System has been installed at the station with the intent to minimise the potential damage caused by a direct lightning strike. All extraneous roof metals <including flues, handrails, walkways, duct supports, mechanical plant and roof lights> have been bonded together using a network of 25mm x 3mm conductor tape. The down conductor network is provided in the form of a series of 25mm x 3mm PVC sheathed aluminium down conductor tapes fitted vertically along the Cardington Street/Melton Street and Eversholt Street elevations. Earth Termination is generally formed by 2.4m long x 16mm diameter steel cored copper clad earth electrodes having been driven into predetermined positions and connected to the down conductor using 25mm x 3mm PVC sheathed copper conductor tape running underground into high performance polymer inspection pits with lockable covers at street level.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Contractors should note that extensive drainage runs exist throughout the Station at all levels. Records of drainage pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: The main water is supplied by Thames Water and is distributed throughout the station. A red coloured fire main in the basement supplies a network of smaller pipes which in turn supply water sprinklers in this area. The sprinklers are automatically activated in the event of fire. Records of water supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Gas supply pipelines are present in many areas of the station although it is not clear whether comprehensive records of the distribution layout exist. Limited records of gas supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency contact 0800 111 999. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Asbestos hazards please see ARMS for specific details

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Man-made-mineral-fibre <mmmf> is a generic term for fibreglass, Rockwool and similar materials which have in recent years been used to replace asbestos as an insulating and structural material. The use and presence of mmmf is not without risk and it is considered as a hazardous substance under the terms of the COSHH Regulations. An assessment should be undertaken to establish that handling requirements <including repair work to insulation> and atmospheric levels are within prescribed guidelines before any work involving these materials commences. Large amounts of lagging consisting of various types of mmmf are present at Euston.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: The track ballast is contaminated with excrement, general rubbish and in some areas has been subjected to oil spillage. A cleaning contract is in place whereby the track within the station is cleaned twice every week.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: These areas can contain hypodermic syringes and needles.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Multi-Storey car park, taxi area and pick-up area. Narrow passages and stairways lead off the parking areas and are often contaminated with biological hazards <urine, vomit and occasionally blood>. Vagrants sometimes use these areas although access has been made difficult to the alcoves under the stairs by means of locked fencing gates.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: There are a number of areas where there is the likelihood of the presence of vermin, especially rats and pigeons. The worse area is the Parcels Deck at the south end of platform 1 + 2 because of unoccupied and infrequently used of these areas. A pest control contractor regularly visits the station and all contractors should be aware that poisoned bait has been put down at various locations.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: A fume extraction alarm system has been installed in the taxi area and will go off, if the levels are to be too high.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Openings in the skylights has been created to get rid of any possible diesel fume from trains.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Access to the roof is restricted to authorised personnel only. The area above the concourse is resurfaced with mineral felt that is very poorly drained and subject to "pooling" in wet weather. This felt roof area can be very slippery and a flagstone path has therefore been made to walk on. Network Rail Management authorisation must be obtained, as should details of any weight or other restrictions, prior to accessing the roof. Care must be taken when entering these areas as there is no edge protection to the upper levels of the roof, particularly over the concourse and control measures need to be identified to reduce the risk of falls, especially when working close to the edge

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Access to the roof is restricted to authorised personnel only. The roof area above the platforms is constructed of aluminium, glass and acrylic panels. Network Rail Management authorisation must be obtained, as should details of any weight or other restrictions, prior to accessing the roof.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: A number of redundant lift shafts to the station platforms below are located in the centre of the Deck. The lift gates are closed and padlocked preventing access and their lower ends, at platform level are, bricked up. Electrical cabinets and heavy associated cable runs are located on the side of at least one of the lift shafts. Platform 16/18 Canteen Lift has now been decommissioned and made safer by the removal of suspended loads, the removal of electrical fuses and `locking off? the supply, as well as the posting of notices. Access into the lift shaft has been prevented by the installation of hasps and padlocks.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Main boiler house PR1, platform 1 has a large high ceiling room contains 2 large oil fired boilers. Flow and return water is pumped around the system by means of electric circulating pumps through a system of pipes, only some of which are insulated and armoured. In the latter case, some of the exposed pipework will be hot when the system is operational. The Boiler Room also contains other services incl. electrical cables, switchgear and fire alarm circuitry. A number of pipe and cable runs are located beneath removable covers running the length of the floor. A metal staircase <4 meters> leads to a Tank Room at a higher level, entered through a doorway at the top of the stairs. This is the only feasible entry/exit, as double doors at the end of the room lead to a drop of some 4m <approx.> to the ramp below. The room contains a large water header tank and ion exchange water softening equipment. It is certainly of limited access and could become a confined space for certain activities

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Below Hardwick House at platform1 - boiler room no. 20. This room contains three large oil fired boilers with large uninsulated flues that will be very hot when the boilers are operational. Two large calorifiers lagged with asbestos containing insulation, and clearly labelled as such, are also present. This insulation is in good condition and the risk from this hazard is minimal providing it is left undisturbed. Should circumstances necessitate its disturbance, the work should not be undertaken except by trained specialists. A large heating control panel, circulating pumps and valve gear are also present together with electrical cable runs and associated switchgear. Fuel oil pipes and electrical cables are contained in a trench in the floor, fitted with a metal cover.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Plant room no. 21 <water>, platform 1 contains a large water header tank supported on a steel girder frame. Water from the tank is used to supply trains from hoses at platform level throughout the station. Water is pumped by means of an electric pump and pressure is maintained in the system when the pump is not working by means of a compressor and connected air reservoir. Pipe runs of varying diameters traverse the room, some of large main water supply pipes are located in a channel in the floor which is covered with an open mesh metal grid. Work in this area should take into consideration the fact that the water systems are under high pressure. Access to the top of the header tank is gained by means of two fixed metal ladders, with a landing. Some pipe runs are located behind the ladders making access to them restricted and for certain work e.g. hot work. This room also contain a large diameter gas main with other gas pipework connected.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Roof of the East side - Plant room no 2. This plant room contains a lot of services and equipment. Special care must be taken as some electrical motors are driven by belts and entanglement could happen. Also special care must be taken as parts of the a/c ductwork is fitted very low and there is a substantial risk of head injury through bumping into it, head protection should therefore be worn. Similarly, the floor is covered in obstructions, many of which are fixed and great care must be taken when negotiating the area, especially away from walkways. There are many areas where it would be difficult to manoeuvre and carry out even simple tasks and these fall into the category of Limited Access. The floor is often wet due to leakage of rainwater through the flat roof and can cause the floor to be slippery.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Roof of the West side - Plant room no 3. This large plant room contains a lot of services and equipment. Special care must be taken as some electrical motors are driven by belts and danger of entanglement if guards are missing could happen. Also special care must be taken as parts of the a/c ductwork is fitted very low and there is a substantial risk of head injury through bumping into it. Head protection should therefore be worn. Similarly, the floor is covered in obstructions, many of which are fixed and great care must be taken when negotiating the area, especially away from walkways. There are many areas where it would be difficult to manoeuvre and carry out even simple tasks and these fall into the category of Limited Access. The floor is often wet due to leakage of rainwater through the flat roof and can cause the floor to be slippery.

Free Text: Ventilation plant room PR4 <mezzanine level via yellow board path>. This room contains a large fan, driven by a guarded belt and electric motor, connected to a large chamber and similarly large ductwork. A hatch allows access into the fan chamber that is covered in a black, sooty, dust and respiratory protective equipment is advised to protect from inhalation of this hazard. The room also contains an electrical control panel and switches, lagged armoured pipework and a water header tank.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Basement <off east trucking way> - Sprinkler Chamber PR19. This room contains the source of the Station's fire main network, pumping equipment, valve gear and other associated equipment, maintained at high pressure. A mains gas supply runs through the room which also contains electric supply and fire alarm equipment <sensors, cables, sprinklers etc.>. At various times there is standing water over areas of the Sprinkler Chamber floor. Possible sources appear to be a leaking bund at one end of the chamber and blocked/leaking RWPs located in the adjacent corridor. A possible 3rd source would be a leaking valve in the chamber itself. This room should be regarded as a confined space in the event of high-pressure water or gas leaks.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Basement <off east trucking way> - Water meter rooms MR2 & MR3. This room contains a water meter connected to large diameter water mains which are lagged <fibreglass?> and mainly armoured, and valve gear. The above gas main and heavy electrical cables and switchgear also pass through the area. This room should be regarded as a confined space in the event of high-pressure water or gas leaks.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Basement <off east trucking way>? - Fan room PR13. This room contains two extract fans, motors and guarded belt drives connected to large ductwork. Typical other plant room services and associated hazards are located in the room, including a fire control panel. Oil leaks on the floor present slip hazards

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Between the main boiler room and basement roadway - Carbon dioxide <CO2> room. Access to this room is strictly limited to authorised and trained personnel only. It contains CO2 gas cylinders and associated pipework, the gas being used to flood the boiler and tank rooms in the event of fire. Clearly, as a precautionary measure to guard against unexpected CO2 leakage, air <oxygen> monitoring equipment is necessary for those requiring access. Please note that CO2 is an asphyxiant gas and <contrary to popular belief> is toxic.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Hardwick, PR20a, near to boiler room no. 20 - Oil tank room. This room is accessed by a small doorway set in the wall some 1.5m <approx.> above platform level, necessitating a drop to floor level once inside; exiting is therefore not easy. A ladder is fixed to the side of the oil storage tank which might be slippery from oil spillage. The room contains an obvious flammable hazard as well as one of toxic vapour which might be evolved from oil spillage etc. A hatch covering valve gear is located on the outside wall adjacent to the entry door.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Platform 1 <rear of main boiler room> - Oil tank room. This room located to the rear of the main boiler room is accessed from the adjacent ramp. It contains three large oil storage tanks of obviously flammable material from which toxic vapour could evolve e.g. during filling, spillage, venting etc.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Multi-Storey car park level 1 - Sump Pump room PR18a. This room contains large diameter drainage pipes, passing through the floor and associated valve gear. Electrical cables <415v> and armoured, lagged pipes are also present. A fixed metal ladder surrounded by a guard on the surface, leads to a sump some 3m <approx.> below the surface. This room is a classic as a confined space and regulations must be followed when entry is room.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Basement <adjacent to train cleaners office> - Fan AHU PR8. This room contains a fan and heat exchanger connected across ductwork which supplies warm air to the premises above. The far end of the duct contains a small hatch of approximately 1m x 0.7m where access could be gained into what is a confined space.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Multi-Storey car park level 1 - Fan room PR18. This room contains two large, electric motor and belt driven <guarded>, impellers in housings of approximately 2m diameter <ext.> connected to air ducts. Electrical cables and switchgear are also present. Several manhole covers which appear to have been recently lifted, are located in the floor; it is unclear what lies below them. Massive filters are located at one end of the room and a doorway leads to the other side of these, accessing a major ventilation shaft apparently stretching the full height of the car park <6 levels>. Fixed metal ladders, interspersed with gridded metal platforms lead to the top of the shaft - some very considerable height. Clearly, great care must be taken if negotiating this route, as a fall would be fatal. This area is of limited access and a permit to work system should be used for those requiring access.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Multi-Storey car park level 4 - Fan room PR16. This room contains two large disused impellers and associated drive gear, ducts etc. similar to PR18. A chamber is located to the rear of the impellers to which ductwork is connected and the impeller fans are open to the chamber. This is a confined space where there would appear to be great danger if the fans are switched on with someone inside. Assessment of work activities in here is essential. Noise and fumes <particulate and gaseous> also need assessing if this has not already been done as the presence of hearing defenders and respiratory protective equipment are present in a cabinet on the wall of the room. The room also contains major electrical switchgear, associated cables, cabinets, lagged and armoured pipework, and fire alarm cables.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Multi-Storey car park level 4 - Fan room PR17. The contents of this room are similar to PR16, except that there is no major switchgear. A balcony fitted with guard-rails drops to a landing some 4m <approx.> below. This is another confined space the purpose of which is not clear although it might be part of a ventilation shaft Two large diameter pipes connected at one end, and with an electric pump and valve gear are located at the end of the room. The purpose of these pipes is unclear but they would appear to be associated with heating or hot water supply due to the presence of lagging <fibreglass?> on them.

ELR Name: LONDON EUSTON - RUGBY TRENT VALLEY JCN
Free Text: Multi-Storey car park level 5 - Fan room 17a. This room is entered through a door which is hermetically sealed giving access to a chamber containing two large fans <although smaller than those in PR17>. The chamber seems to form an integral part of the air ducting itself. At the end of the chamber is a very long duct of approximately 0.75m width x 3m height which appears to run the length of the car park and it appears that fume extraction points are located off this duct. This whole system is a confined space and just one important aspect is to protect against personnel becoming shut in.

Free Text: Vehicular Access Point with 10 parking spaces. Access via HS2 controlled gate (Gate 18 off Hampstead Road by Bus stop and signed) Staff to show Sentinel card to HS2 security to be granted access. Once through teams can enter NR access point via No7 lock on black double gates. Egress is via HS2 MDJV site Back up the ramp and into Cardington Street and follow the signs through the MDJV site then out to exit via Melton Street Melton Street. Between the hours of 07:00hrs and 19:00hrs MDJV staff managed Gate 18 continuously between these hours to allow maintenance staff to “turn up and access” without prior notice. Egress via Melton Street Between the hours of 19:00hrs and 07:00hrs Advanced notification required from NR Maintenance staff by contacting the Project Security Control Room (SCR) which is operated 24/7 by Mace Dragados (MDjv) on Mobile Number 07977 156687 Once the SCR had been contacted the MDjv site operatives will be available within a 10-minute period to manage controlled access through the Site Areas. Egress via HS2/MDJV site then out to Melton Street and on to Euston Road.During PossessionsNR provided a week by week view of possessions between Euston and Camden Junction. MDJV implemented enhanced security during possessions that included high number platforms (which required access from the PSB access point). No need to phone ahead during booked possession times only.

Free Text:
Hot wire working in place on the CRC2. 
Location CRC2: 5m42ch to 5m61ch From: Structures V00/01 (Up Primrose Hill Line) and G01/102 (Down Primrose Hill Line) To V01/12 (Up & Down Primrose Hill Lines)
AC Electrical Sections: AE-3V & AE-11 (Up Primrose Hill line) AE-3U & AE-12 (Down Primrose Hill line)
DC Electrical Section sections: 904D, E (Down North London DC Electric) 903C, D (Up North London DC Electric)
Working within the limits: requirements below :
Transit through the limits: Allowed under possession, line blockage (including a line blockage using signal protection where authorised). No traction isolation is required for transit. BUT BOTH UP AND DOWN MUST BE BLOCKED AT THE SAME TIME

Low‑level work within the limits is permitted with the OLE de‑energised and the appropriate DC isolation and permits in place. A full isolation with earths applied is not required for low‑level work unless the task risk assessment dictates it. Low‑level work means tasks that do not require a MEWP, ladder, or other elevated platform. This does not override permit or competence requirements.
•
High‑level work within the limits requires a full isolation in line with Network Rail standards and policies.
•
Isolation times for AC and DC do not need to mirror outside the work activity, but during any period when staff are working within the limits, both systems must be de‑energised.
•
Examples of “working”: tightening, cutting or disconnecting rails, cables, or bonds; any task that requires stopping within the limits to undertake a physical activity. These count as working.
•
Encroaching assets: If equipment outside the HWW limits encroaches into, or terminates within, the limits, treat it as within the HWW area and apply the relevant rule above.
•
An isolation may be granted on DC sections 903 or 904 without isolating AE-3U, AE-3V, AE - 11 or AE-12, provided that no work is taking place within 903C, 903D, 904D, or 904E

Inert and excavation waste. Site restored in 1992. Believed to have previously been used for domestic waste. Tip is approximately 250 metres from this line on opposite side of Willesden Yard. 
Landfill gas present? No 
Hazards associated with sites within 250m of known landfill include:
Hazardous gases
Leachate (hazardous liquids)
Contaminated soils 
Project sponsors shall request landfill information for all proposed construction projects (NR/L3/CIV/066 Clause 18).
Request form can be found on Technical Authority’s Mining Team Connect page: 
https://networkrail.sharepoint.com/sites/nrmyconnect-technicalauthority/SitePages/Mining.aspx
For further advice, contact nationalminingengineer@networkrail.co.uk
